title: How to get the absolute value (and sum absolute values)
task: Strip the sign from numbers — distances, error magnitudes, reconciling debits and credits.

excel.note: Sum a whole range's magnitudes in one cell: =SUMPRODUCT(ABS(A2:A10)).

explanation: ABS drops the sign: −37.5 becomes 37.5, and positives pass through unchanged. The common follow-on is totaling magnitudes regardless of direction — =SUMPRODUCT(ABS(A2:A10)) does it without a helper column (SUM(ABS(range)) alone won't work in older Excel without Ctrl+Shift+Enter). Also handy in comparisons: ABS(A2-B2)<=0.01 tests whether two numbers are within a tolerance, the robust way to compare floats.
